specification.topic-statement: The tool argument parser extracts typed values from JSON-encoded tool call argument strings.

scope.in-scope: Parsing a JSON object string and extracting either a string-typed or integer-typed field value by key name.
scope.boundary: The argument string is parsed into an in-memory document by a JSON library. This topic drives the parse and field lookup but does not implement the parsing.
Extracted string values are duplicated into a caller-provided allocation context. This topic initiates the allocation; ownership of the returned string belongs to that context.

field.name: allocation context
field.note: talloc context; may be null for root allocation

field.name: success flag
field.note: true if value was written, false on any failure

field.name: value
field.note: written to output location only on success; untouched on failure

behavior.string-extraction:
Extracting a string value from a JSON argument string follows these steps in order, returning null at the first failure:
If the argument string or the key is null, null is returned immediately without attempting any parsing.
The argument string is parsed as JSON. If parsing fails (malformed JSON, empty input, or other read error), null is returned.
The root value of the parsed document must be a JSON object. If the root is absent or is any other type (array, string, number, boolean, or null), the document is freed and null is returned.
The requested key is looked up in the root object. If the key is not present, the document is freed and null is returned.
The value associated with the key must be a JSON string. If it is any other type (number, boolean, object, array, or null), the document is freed and null is returned.
The string content is duplicated into the caller-provided allocation context. The JSON document is freed. The duplicated string is returned.
If the allocation fails, the process terminates immediately (out-of-memory is treated as an unrecoverable condition). This path is the only unrecoverable failure mode.
Null is returned for any failure — missing key, wrong type, invalid JSON, or null input — with no indication of which condition occurred.
behavior.integer-extraction:
Extracting an integer value from a JSON argument string follows the same parse-and-lookup sequence, returning false at the first failure:
If the argument string, the key, or the output location is null, false is returned immediately without parsing.
The argument string is parsed as JSON. If parsing fails, false is returned.
The root must be a JSON object. Any other root type causes the document to be freed and false to be returned.
The key is looked up in the root object. If absent, the document is freed and false is returned.
The value must be a JSON integer type. Floating-point numbers, strings, booleans, objects, arrays, and null all cause the document to be freed and false to be returned.
The integer value is read as a signed 64-bit integer. The document is freed. The 64-bit value is cast to a platform-native integer and written to the output location. True is returned.
The 64-bit value is cast to a native integer without range validation. If the JSON integer value exceeds the maximum or minimum value representable by a native integer, the stored result is silently truncated. No error is returned.
False is returned for any failure — missing key, wrong type, invalid JSON, null parameters — with no indication of which condition occurred. The output location is not written on failure.
